Ad Text:
About the Role
We’re looking for an experienced and commercially minded Product & Optimisation Lead (Senior BA) to drive continuous improvement across our contact centre platforms and customer operations ecosystem.

In this role, you’ll own and manage the optimisation backlog across CCaaS, Salesforce, and related capabilities — ensuring business needs are translated into practical, prioritised solutions that improve operational performance, user adoption, customer experience, and business value.

You’ll work closely with operations, sales, technology, data teams, and external vendors to shape initiatives, define requirements, coordinate delivery, and support successful implementation outcomes.

This is a highly collaborative role suited to someone who combines strong business analysis capability with product thinking, stakeholder management, and a passion for operational optimisation.

Key Responsibilities

Own and manage the optimisation backlog across CCaaS, Salesforce, and supporting contact centre capabilities 
Translate commercial and operational needs into clear requirements, user stories, process improvements, and prioritised delivery initiatives 
Lead discovery sessions, process mapping, and solution design across workflows, routing, reporting, and platform enhancements 
Support roadmap planning, quarterly prioritisation, and release governance activities across optimisation initiatives 
Work closely with platform leads, operations, sales, technology, data teams, and vendors to coordinate delivery and manage dependencies 
Coordinate UAT, implementation support, and change readiness activities for new capabilities and enhancements 
Track adoption, benefits realisation, and operational impact to ensure initiatives deliver measurable value 
Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reduce rework, and strengthen overall platform effectiveness 
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience in a Senior Business Analyst, Product Owner, Optimisation, or Continuous Improvement role 
Strong experience working within contact centre environments and platforms such as CCaaS and Salesforce 
Demonstrated capability in backlog management, requirements gathering, process mapping, and solution delivery 
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence across operational and technical teams 
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and prioritisation skills 
Experience supporting UAT, change management, and implementation activities 
Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on delivery execution 
Success in This Role Looks Like

A clear and prioritised optimisation backlog aligned to business and customer outcomes 
Improved delivery quality from requirements through to implementation 
Increased adoption and effective use of contact centre and CRM capabilities 
Measurable operational improvements and value realisation from optimisation initiatives 
Reduced rework and stronger delivery outcomes through better process design and stakeholder alignment 
High levels of stakeholder confidence in prioritisation, delivery clarity, and business readiness 
Why Join Us?

Opportunity to shape and optimise key customer and operational platforms 
Collaborative and fast-paced environment with strong cross-functional exposure 
High-impact role with visibility across operations, technology, and customer experience 
Ongoing opportunities for growth, innovation, and professional development 
Apply Now
If you’re passionate about optimisation, product thinking, and delivering practical business improvements, we’d love to hear from you.

Technical Lead : Enterprise Financial Systems
  
We are seeking an experienced Technical Lead specialising in Financial Systems & ERP Platforms (SAP) to lead the ongoing optimisation, governance, support and enhancement of Enterprise Finance Systems across a complex organisational environment.
  
This is a senior leadership role suited to someone who combines deep SAP ECC6 capability with strong stakeholder engagement, technical configuration skills and people leadership. You will play a pivotal role in ensuring financial systems are stable, scalable, integrated and aligned to strategic business objectives while leading a high-performing team through continuous improvement and transformation initiatives.
  
About the Role:
  
Reporting to the General Manager Business Performance, you will be responsible for leading the finance systems function across ERP platforms, business intelligence tools and reporting environments.
You will provide both strategic and hands-on leadership across:

SAP ECC6 configuration, maintenance and optimisation
ERP integrations and financial systems governance
Financial data integrity, controls and audit support
Systems upgrades, enhancements and implementations
Business process improvement and automation
Data governance and reporting structures
Stakeholder engagement across finance, technology and executive teams
Leadership, mentoring and development of a specialist systems team
This role requires someone who can bridge the gap between finance and technology, bringing a commercially minded and technically capable approach to enterprise systems leadership.
  
Key Technical Requirements:
  
To be successful in this role, you will ideally have:

Extensive experience with SAP ECC6 including strong configuration and integration capability across:
FICO (Financial Accounting & Controlling)
MM (Materials Management)
SD (Sales & Distribution)
PS (Project Systems)
Strong systems accounting and ERP leadership experience
Proven experience managing enterprise finance applications and integrations
Expertise in financial data governance, controls and reporting structures
Experience supporting and enhancing BI, CPM and reporting platforms
Advanced analytical capability including complex data analysis and reporting
Experience leading system upgrades, testing, implementations and transformation initiatives
Exposure to Microsoft Dynamics AX/D365 and business intelligence/reporting platforms will be highly regarded, but not mandatory.
  
Leadership Capability
  
You are a collaborative and engaging people leader who can:

Lead and develop high-performing technical teams
Influence stakeholders across finance, technology and executive leadership
Drive change and systems transformation initiatives
Translate technical concepts into practical business outcomes
Build strong partnerships across complex organisational environments
Balance strategic thinking with operational delivery
  
About You
You will ideally possess:

Minimum 5+ years’ experience in a senior finance systems or systems accounting leadership role
Demonstrated experience operating within large, complex enterprise environments
Relevant qualifications in Accounting, Business, Information Technology or related discipline
  
This is an opportunity to step into a highly visible leadership role where you can shape the future direction of enterprise financial systems and drive meaningful transformation outcomes. You’ll join a collaborative and forward-thinking environment that values innovation, continuous improvement and strong technical leadership.

Investing in you - benefits package including salary packaging, health and wellbeing programs, discounted services, and professional development opportunities.

The University of Melbourne
We take pride in our people, who all contribute to our mission to benefit society through the transformative impact of education and research. Discover more via our website.

Your next career opportunity
As a Senior Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to design, build, and maintain robust data pipelines within the University's Enterprise Data Lake. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to support advanced analytics and machine learning, ensuring seamless data integration and high performance. Your expertise will be pivotal in driving data governance and security, contributing to a transformative data environment.

Your responsibilities will include:

Architect, built and own production data pipelines supporting University wide analytics and reporting, ensuring reliability, performance and data quality.
Lead technical design for data integration projects, translating business requirements into scalable solutions with full delivery accountability.
Mentor and develop data engineering members, establishing coding standards, conducting design reviews, fostering excellence.
Define and evolve data engineering best practices, evaluating emerging technologies and influencing technical direction.

You may be a great fit if:
You are an experienced data engineer with deep expertise in Snowflake and dbt, a track record of leading and mentoring engineering teams, and solid experience with AWS technologies, adept at leading pipeline development and optimising data storage and retrieval, while collaborating effectively in cross-functional teams.

You may also:

Have 10 or more years of experience in data engineering, particularly with Snowflake and dbt in production environments
Demonstrate deep hands-on experience with Snowflake (architecture, performance tuning, data modelling) and dbt (modelling, testing, documentation, CI/CD integration).
Possess strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing and transformation.
Strong experience with AWS services (S3, Glue, Lambda, CDK) and CI/CD pipelines.
Exhibit excellent problem-solving skills for optimising data pipeline performance.
Be skilled in communicating complex data engineering concepts to diverse stakeholders.

For further information, please refer to the attached PD.

We offer the opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits to support your success and sense of fulfilment, including:

Supportive flexible work arrangements underpinned by our commitment to inclusion and well-being .
Progressive, considerate leave provisions to empower your work-life balance
Salary packaging and access to a range of discounted services including Bupa health insurance
Health and well-being services including a leading Employee Assistance Program

For more information check out our benefits page!

Enterprise Services Group delivers functions that facilitate the design, evaluation, and improvement of services across the University, and business partnering services to Chancellery. At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

That’s why we pledge to empower all of our people to make a positive impact.  Combining our deep technical expertise with the capabilities of our clients and partners, we respond to some of the most complex challenges facing our planet today.

Who are we looking for? 

We’re seeking an experienced Spatial Analyst to join our South-East Queensland Location Intelligence team. This role suits someone who combines strong technical capability with the confidence to engage meaningfully with clients and stakeholders. You’ll bring a genuine curiosity for the evolving spatial industry and the judgement to apply your skills thoughtfully as technologies and client requirements continue to shift.

This role can be based in either Brisbane, Gold Coast, Sunshine Coast or Toowoomba. 

Working with an energetic and high-performing team, you will: 

Develop and deliver geospatial and data science products and services, ensuring compliance with quality assurance standards. 
Engage proactively with clients, with support from the LI Team Lead, to identify insights and opportunities contributing to the team’s pipeline targets and future business development. 
Collaborate with technical leads across the business to support GHD’s business goals and KPIs. 
Contribute to the growth and successful performance of LI capabilities, including workflow design and automation, solution implementation, and administration of spatial databases and web mapping and dashboarding environments. 
Lead proposal preparation, including input collation, writing, and technical documentation. 

What you will bring to the team: 

Bachelor's degree in Spatial Science, or equivalent with a minimum 7 years of professional experience in similar industries.
Highly developed oral, written and visual communication skills, with an emphasis on being able to convey ideas and outcomes to a broad range of stakeholders.
Strong spatial analysis skills with the ability to work across software solutions
Data management skills, including an understanding of geospatial data models, schemas and standards.
Data collection form design and development with an eye to optimising for user experience and automation
Demonstrated experience in the use of AI technology in an industry use case
The ability to design and develop automated workflow solutions
Proficiency in ESRI GIS Software Suite
Experience in Technical Project Management, Client Engagement, and Relationship Management. 

Preferable experience in: 

GeoAI workflows and solutions
GIS Development Skills
FME
Technical Business Analysis
PowerBI
Data Modelling 

 
And If you are the unicorn who happens to have both Spatial and Data Science skillsets, we’d love to hear from you.
 
Interested? Apply now.

GHD is an equal opportunity employer, and we want everyone to be able to fully participate in our recruitment process. We value a diverse workforce and an inclusive culture. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and people of all identities. 

GHD embraces hybrid work arrangements to create a work environment focused on well-being, flexibility, and productivity where our people can thrive. This supports the diverse needs of our workforce while balancing the benefits of remote working with the value of face-to-face collaboration in the office. 

We respectfully ask that no agency resumes be presented at this stage.

ROLE SUMMARY

In this role, you will act as the critical link between day‑to‑day finance and operational activities and CRE's broader digital transformation agenda. With a strong grounding in ERP systems, data models and business process mapping, you will partner with finance, operations and technology stakeholders to improve business processes, system integrations, reporting capability and provide insights.

You will work across a modern, multi‑system environment to design, enhance and maintain automated, system‑led processes for financial reporting, analysis, budgeting and forecasting. This is a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role requiring strong analytical capability, commercial thinking and the ability to translate technical solutions into value-add business outcomes.

ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES

Systems, Data & Integrations

-       Maintain system integrations

-       Manage system security/access

-       Design data models

-       Ensure data accuracy

Reporting, Analytics & Insights

-       Build BI dashboards

-       Automate reporting processes

-       Define analytics data frameworks

-       Generate value-add insights

Stakeholder Engagement

-       Partner with business stakeholders

-       Train and support users

-       Liaise with system consultants

Systems & Process Improvement

-       Document end-to-end processes

-       Resolve system data issues

-       Gather user systems feedback

-       Continuous process improvement

-       Continuous systems improvement

YOUR CAPABILITY

You are commercially minded, analytically strong and naturally curious about how technology can improve business performance. You are comfortable working across both finance and systems domains and thrive in environments where you can translate technical detail into clear business impact.

You bring a proactive mindset, strong work ethic and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment. Collaborative and approachable, you engage confidently with stakeholders across the business, anticipate issues before they arise and propose practical, system‑led solutions. You take pride in delivering high‑quality outcomes, pay close attention to detail and enjoy contributing to initiatives that drive lasting improvement.

Professional Experience 

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Information Systems, Commerce or a related discipline.

3–6 years’ experience in a finance analyst or systems analyst role within a multi‑system environment.

Hands‑on experience with ERP systems and data platforms, with exposure to Sage Intacct and Snowflake highly regarded.

Demonstrated experience building and maintaining Power BI models or other business intelligence & data visualisation tools.

Experience using scripting or automation tools to improve reporting or finance processes.

Strong understanding of relational databases, data modelling and process flow design.

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and strong familiarity with Microsoft 365 tools including PowerPoint, SharePoint, Visio and Excel.

Strong communication, time‑management and project delivery capability, with the ability to work both independently and as part of a team.

COMPANY SUMMARY

CRE Group is a leading civil construction and material processing contractor operating across Victoria, New South Wales, South Australia, and Queensland. Our company continues to grow rapidly, with projects diversified across civil construction, bulk earthworks, remediation works, landfill construction, and crushing & screening services.

We are committed to delivering excellence in our industry, driven by our core values of culture, respect, and excellence. Our people are collaborative, hardworking and supportive. Their commitment to the company’s success fuels our growth, and in turn, creates opportunities within CRE Group for our people.

Our federal government and private sector clients are looking for TSPV Data Specialists to deliver and maintain Australia's strategic advantage and capability edge over the coming decade. Your work will ensure that our nation remains one step ahead, equipped with the insights necessary to safeguard our interests at home and abroad.

Working on top-secret projects and having access to cutting-edge technology are among the exciting prospects that contractors can look forward to. As is the security of long-term work in programs with a wide range of job opportunities for individuals with various skills and expertise. These roles are based in Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane and requires an Australian Top Secret Positive Vetting (TSPV) security clearance. These opportunities do not have the ability to obtain/upgrade sponsorship for this security clearance.

These are initial 12-month contracts with extension options.

Estimated start, late 2026.

Whether you're ready to take the next step in your career or simply exploring new opportunities, we encourage you to apply and or reach out to myself directly for a confidential discussion.

We are looking for all backgrounds and levels of experience - Junior, Mid, and Senior!

Database Administrators
Data Engineers
Data Scientists

Kinexus has been supplying contractors to Federal Government panels since 2004 and have a strong relationship with them.
Our experience in managing contractors in defence/national security industries means we understand the requirements from the Commonwealth, insurances and clearances.
DISP accredited; we can hold clearances and manage renewals.

Head of Research, Property

This is not a typical research role.

At Blue Wealth Property, research is the foundation of everything we do. It underpins every recommendation, every property we select, and the trust we build with our clients and business partners.

We are now looking for a Head of Research to lead this function, elevate it further, and ensure it continues to set the standard in the market.

This is a career-defining role for the right person.

Why Blue Wealth Property

At Blue Wealth Property, you’ll be joining one of Australia’s most trusted property research and advisory groups. With over 17 years in the market, our reputation has been built on the strength, integrity and consistency of our research.

Our methodology is independently audited – something that sets us apart, and reflects our commitment to delivering disciplined, research-backed property decisions. Research is not a support function here; it is at the core of our business.

You’ll be working alongside a high-performing, collaborative team in a business where research is valued, respected, and central to every outcome. This is an environment where your thinking will be heard, your expertise will be trusted, and your work will directly influence the success of our clients, partners, and the business.

The Role

You will take ownership of the research function across the business, leading the team, refining our methodology, and ensuring research is consistently driving confident, high-quality decisions.

Your role will span leadership, strategy, and execution.

You will:

Lead and develop the research team to deliver high-quality, timely support across the business

Provide clear, research-backed justification to support Sales, BDMs, Client Services, and our Business Partners

Own and evolve our proprietary research methodology to ensure it remains best in market

Influence which properties we select - and which we don’t - based on strong research conviction

Produce high-quality research content including annual reports, event material, blogs, and client-facing insights

Present at events and become a trusted voice of research within the business and to our partners

Educate and uplift the broader team, building confidence and consistency in how research is understood and applied

Monitor and report on the performance of property recommendations over time

About You

You are a commercial research leader who understands how insight drives outcomes.

You bring:

Experience in property research

Strong commercial instinct - you understand how research translates into decisions and results

The ability to simplify complex data into clear, compelling narratives

Confidence to present, influence, and challenge thinking

Leadership capability - you can build, guide and elevate a team

High standards, ownership, and accountability

You are decisive, pragmatic, and solutions focused. You bring energy, presence, and conviction to your role.

If you are looking for a role where your thinking drives real outcomes - and where you can build something meaningful, this is it.

$140,000 - $170,000 + Superannuation + Salary Packaging ($15,900.00 + Meal & Entertainment expenses)
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements

About the Institute
Located on the lands of the Bidiagal Clan of the Dharawal Nation (Randwick, NSW), Black Dog Institute is the only Medical Research Institute in Australia to investigate mental health across the lifespan, our aim is to create a mentally healthier world for everyone. For the past 20 years, we have been at the forefront of mental health research. Our work is used to drive evidence informed change across the rapidly expanding mental health sector and sets the foundations for a brighter and healthier future for all.

Benefits

Hybrid work, 1-2 days per week in office (choose your days, consistent team collaboration days where possible)
35 hour work week, flexible hours agreements & 9-day fortnights
Salary Packaging: $15,900.00 of your base salary tax free & up to $2650.00 meal & entertainment
Family first, people first culture, we put our research into action at work to make sure we maintain mental health and wellbeing at work
We in the Innovate stage of our Reconcilation Action Plan (RAP), actively embedding reconciliation into our workplace culture.
Pet friendly workplace
Leave benefits including public holiday swap, gifted annual leave, and wellbeing leave 
Employee discounts and gym membership benefits



The Role

The Head of Enterprise Portfolio Office provides strategic leadership for Black Dog Institute’s (BDI’s) enterprise‑wide portfolio, program and project management capability. This role is responsible for establishing a high‑performing Enterprise Portfolio Office, embedding strong governance, and driving the effective execution of BDI’s 2030 strategy. As a trusted partner to the Executive Leadership Team, the Head of EPO leads organisational planning, prioritisation and implementation processes, ensuring initiatives are sequenced, resourced, governed, and delivered effectively across research, clinical, operational and strategic domains. This role is also responsible for implementing safe and ethical use of AI in the EPO.

Duties & Responsibilities

Portfolio & Program Management

Oversee the management of BDI's enterprise strategic portfolio
Build and embed portfolio management processes including prioritisation, stage-gate reviews, benefits realisation, and executive/board reporting
Oversee coordination of designated ‘transformational’ initiatives, ensuring interdependencies are identified and managed
Provide expert advice to executives on program structuring, planning, governance and delivery
Resolve portfolio level risks and resource conflicts

Strategy Implementation, Monitoring & Governance

Translate BDI's 2030 strategy into actionable annual and multi-year implementation plans
Drive annual planning and organisational prioritisation cycles including business case development, portfolio reviews and forward planning
Oversee the development and continuous improvement of enterprise governance frameworks, operating models, methodologies and delivery standards.
Oversee the establishment and maintenance of an organisational impact framework to measure and monitor delivery against strategic objectives
Ensure timely, meaningful and insightful reporting to ELT, Board and governance committees
Provide strategic advice on organisational transformation and change

EPO Leadership & People Management

Lead, manage, and develop a high performing EPO team
Build a strong community of practice and promote uplift in project and program management maturity across the organisation.
Oversee the optimisation and consistent adoption of standardised project management approaches
Implement the ethical and safe use of AI in EPO and strategy activities
Provide targeted advisory and delivery support agreed initiatives

Executive, Board & Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a trusted advisor to senior executives, sponsors, and governance bodies
Build transparent, trusted relationships across research, clinical, and corporate functions.
Facilitate governance forums, portfolio reviews, and reporting cycles
Ensure stakeholder engagement and program delivery activities align with BDI's enterprise governance frameworks

Financial & Resource Management

Oversee risk, issue and dependency management across the portfolio.
Support business cases, cost–benefit analysis and resource planning to inform decision‑making.
Monitor project and portfolio budgets, ensuring transparency and responsible stewardship.
Advise on resource capacity and scenario planning across the portfolio.
Support grant and funding submissions with program planning expertise.

Work Health & Safety

Ensure self and staff comply with WHS legislation, WHS obligations and internal policies.
Report any WHS hazards and significant issues to Director, Operations, People & Culture or delegate and via the Reporting tool Rely.
Work in a safe manner, applying a duty of care
Contribute to a safe, supportive and mentally healthy working environment consistent with Black Dog Institute values.

This role description is not intended to be exhaustive and may evolve in response to organisational priorities and transformation needs.

Skills & Experience Required 

Essential Criteria

Tertiary qualification in business, project management, or related field
Extensive experience (8+ years) in senior project/program management roles, including establishing or leading an EPMO, EPO similar function
Experience as a People Manager, leading people and teams
Proven track record delivering complex strategic initiatives
Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to improve work quality and efficiency,
Demonstrated experience in enterprise risk management, governance frameworks, and strategic planning
Strong capability in program governance, operating models and executive-level reporting.
Proven ability to engage effectively with senior executives, Boards and cross-functional stakeholders.
High level of judgement, strategic thinking and problem-solving capability in complex environments.
Ability to manage sensitive and confidential matters with discretion and integrity.
 

Desirable Criteria

Formal project management certification (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2, Agile)
Practical experience in leveraging AI within the Microsoft 365 environment and/or Atlassian products (e.g. Jira, Confluence)
Experience in NFP, health, medical research, or mental health sectors
Knowledge of research operations, clinical program management, or academic environments

Inclusion at BDI  

Black Dog Institute is a Sunflower endorsed organisation 

We are committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience where all candidates can fully participate in the process.

We warmly encourage applications from First Nations peoples, culturally and racially diverse communities, LGBTQIA+ individuals, people with disabilities, and others from underrepresented backgrounds.

We recognise that not everyone has had equal access to opportunities, and valuable skills and perspectives can be gained through diverse life experiences.

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.  

The Senior Digital Insights & Reporting Analyst plays a key role in supporting Bank Australia’s Digital & Experience function by delivering high‑quality digital analytics, reporting and performance insights across the CMO division. The role transforms data from multiple sources into actionable intelligence that enables customer‑centred, data‑driven decision‑making across Digital, Brand and Marketing teams. Through automated reporting solutions and proactive performance monitoring, the role identifies opportunities to optimise digital channels, enhance customer engagement and support the bank’s growth objectives, while ensuring continuous improvement and compliance across customer‑facing digital platforms.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Deliver accurate, timely reporting and dashboards across digital channels (website, app, digital banking and acquisition funnels) to provide visibility of performance and customer behaviour.
Lead CRM performance reporting across email, SMS, app and internet banking, analysing lifecycle programs to improve engagement, conversion and retention.
Analyse digital journeys, campaigns and customer behaviour to identify trends, opportunities and areas for optimisation.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and executive‑ready narratives that support digital experience and growth objectives.
Build and enhance automated reporting solutions using tools such as Power BI, Google tools and SQL, reducing manual effort and improving efficiency.
Support consistent integration, tracking and governance of digital data sources, ensuring high data quality and reliable insights.
Proactively monitor digital performance to identify anomalies, friction points or emerging issues, supporting root‑cause analysis and resolution.
Ensure analytics and reporting comply with privacy, regulatory and data‑governance standards, maintaining clear documentation and audit support.
Partner closely with internal teams and external analytics and development partners to deliver ad‑hoc insights, deep dives and ongoing performance reporting.

What you will bring

Degree in data analytics, information systems, marketing analytics or related discipline.
Strong experience in digital analytics, BI or reporting roles.
Experience supporting digital channels (websites, mobile apps or online banking), with knowledge of tagging, CX or marketing analytics desirable.
Experience in regulated environments (e.g. banking or financial services) highly regarded.
High attention to detail and commitment to data integrity, privacy and quality standards.
Demonstrated experience building automated, interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI, Looker or similar BI tools.
Practical experience using digital analytics platforms (e.g. Google Analytics or Adobe Analytics) to interpret behavioural and performance data.
Strong data capability including advanced Excel, SQL, and exposure to data modelling, DAX and reporting automation tools.
Proven ability to translate complex digital data into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical audiences.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a collaborative approach and ability to deliver to deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

By submitting an application you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. At Rabobank, our commitment to our employees and clients is at the heart of everything we do.

As the world’s leading specialist in food and agribusiness banking, one of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture and are committed to adding long-term value for clients.

Our culture is unique, because every day our people work knowing that we are all playing our part in supporting our farmers to feed the world. We strive to create an open, balanced, and flexible workplace where people feel that they belong, can be open and honest, and are supported. We actively encourage collaboration, knowledge sharing, and continuous development, and we are focused on growing each other.

About the role:

RaboResearch is Rabobank’s global knowledge and strategic insights provider. Our global and regional network of analysts are trusted sources of insights for clients, colleagues, policymakers, the industry, and the media -known for independent insights, rigorous analysis, and clear communication on the forces shaping food and agriculture.

The RaboResearch Australia & New Zealand team is looking for a Commodity Analyst to cover some of today’s most dynamic and fast‑moving parts of the agricultural value chain. This role is designed for someone who enjoys learning, collaborating, and contributing ideas in a supportive, high‑impact environment. This is an opportunity to make a difference using your analytical and communication skills and your interest in the food and agribusiness sector.

Based preferably in Sydney (Melbourne or Brisbane might be considered), this role focuses on farm inputs, including fertilisers, agrochemicals, farmland values, and farm machinery—as well as fibres, with a particular emphasis on cotton and wool. These sectors sit at the centre of major structural change, driving price volatility and technology adoption (including AI), while being heavily influenced by geopolitics, climate risk, and evolving global demand.

This is a crucial and influential role supporting the bank and its rural and corporate clients in their short-and long-term strategic decision making. We are looking for a candidate who enjoys combining data, economics, and industry knowledge—and who is equally comfortable writing, presenting, and speaking publicly.

The Analyst will be required to:

Deliver thought provoking, high‑quality market and industry research on (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and fibres (cotton/wool) for internal stakeholders and external clients across Australia and New Zealand

Analyse key drivers such as price volatility, cost structures, technology adoption, regulation, sustainability, and global trade flows

Contribute actively to RaboResearch’s global coverage team, partnering closely with colleagues across the world to produce consistently strong global insights and utilise those locally.

Produce engaging research outputs, including thought‑leadership reports, podcasts, presentations, videos, and digital content, with support from colleagues and established processes.

Act as the sector expert, building credibility and confidence when presenting insights at client events, industry conferences, field days, and internal strategy sessions across Australia and New Zealand

Build a public profile as a trusted voice at a pace that suits your experience and career stage, engaging with print, radio, TV, and digital media on key market and industry developments.

Work closely with Rabobank’s rural and corporate banking teams to support client conversations, identify commercial opportunities, and inform strategic thinking.

Provide research input into credit policy, sector strategy, and risk assessment, including presentations to senior management when required.

You will be encouraged to take ownership of your research while being well supported by a collaborative, trans‑Tasman and global team that covers food and agri research across crops, livestock, and consumer foods. You will benefit from joining a diverse, respectful, and inclusive team of analysts.

To be successful in the role, you will have: 

Bachelor’s Degree in related field required.

Deep industry knowledge, data literacy and technical skills, storytelling and communication and commercial mindset and ability to work across functions/geographies.

A strong analytical mindset and genuine curiosity about how agricultural markets work

Solid understanding of agriculture, agribusiness, or commodity markets gained through professional, academic, or lived experience—ideally with exposure to farm inputs (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and/or fibres (cotton and wool)

Confidence working with large data sets, building models, and interpreting quantitative and qualitative information, with the willingness to keep learning and ask questions.

The ability to translate complex analysis and results in clear messages and a memorable story that engage a wide range of audiences - from conferences and board rooms to kitchen tables on farm.

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability (or ambition) to present to diverse audiences and engage with media.

A client-focused and commercial mindset and the ability to take the perspective of the client and analyze the forces that shape the future of their business.

High proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, and comfort using new technologies and data tools to enhance analysis.

A strong collaborative mindset and willingness to connect the dots by leveraging our global and local network.

Flexibility to travel regularly and represent RaboResearch at client and industry events.

A desire to keep learning and to use technology—including emerging analytical tools—as a competitive advantage.

A positive, open mindset and the confidence to challenge established thinking in a constructive and respectful way, and to deliver clear forward-looking messages and scenarios in times of high uncertainty.

This role is well‑suited for experienced commodity analysts. Equally, this could also be an opportunity for early‑career agricultural economists and data‑driven graduates with a farming background who are eager to deepen their sector knowledge and build a public profile.

If you are early in your career, you will benefit from:

Working alongside experienced RaboResearch analysts in Australia and across our global network

Hands‑on exposure to real‑world market analysis that directly informs client decisions.

Opportunities to develop your writing, presentation, and media engagement skills.

Ongoing coaching, mentorship, feedback and support from local and global peers

We value potential, curiosity, analytical thinking, and the right attitude as much as prior experience. If you’re excited by this role and you are motivation to keep learning, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement listed.

What we're proud to offer you: 

Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home.  

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives. 

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development. 

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave) 

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave isn't enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave. 

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance. 

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners.

Our Values: 

Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven”, “Responsible”, “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds. 

Please let our Talent Acquisition team know if you need any accommodations to make our opportunities more accessible to you. 

Rabobank is not accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role. 

Applicants must hold valid work rights for this role.

About the position

The Associate Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation provides leadership to advance interdisciplinary research and teaching in AI-driven marketing and digital innovation. This position fosters collaboration across disciplines to strengthen the curriculum, ensuring it remains contemporary and relevant to industry trends. The role also supports scholarship that addresses the fast-changing landscape of marketing through AI, data, platforms, and new technologies, with a focus on ethical and responsible use of AI.

The Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation is responsible for advancing La Trobe Business School’s strategic direction in marketing, with a focus on artificial intelligence, digital innovation, and analytics. This position drives curriculum development, high-impact research, and industry engagement, while fostering interdisciplinary collaboration to position the School as a leader in future-focused, industry-relevant education. The Professor is nationally and internationally recognized for contributions to AI-enabled marketing and customer analytics, and is instrumental in preparing graduates for success in evolving, technology-driven environments.

Skills and Experience

The Associate Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation must hold a PhD in Marketing or a related discipline, demonstrate effective academic leadership, and expertise in marketing, digital innovation, and AI-driven practices. The position requires a strong research record, cross-disciplinary collaboration, curriculum innovation, digital learning proficiency, ability to build partnerships and secure funding, postgraduate supervision, mentoring experience, and excellent communication skills for collaboration across academic and professional groups.

The Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation must have a PhD or equivalent in Marketing or a related field, demonstrate effective senior academic leadership, recognized expertise in marketing, digital innovation, and AI, and a strong record of cross-disciplinary collaboration. Essential experience includes a distinguished research profile with national or international recognition, leadership in teaching and curriculum innovation, mentoring experience, and excellent communication skills for collaboration across academic and professional groups.

All La Trobe University employees are bound by the Working with Children Act 2005.  If you are successful, you will be required to hold a valid Victorian Employee Working with Children Check prior to commencement.

National Code on Gender Based Violence in Higher Education

La Trobe University is committed to upholding the National Code to Prevent and Respond to Gender-Based Violence (GBV Code). This aligns with our mission to create safe and respectful communities. Candidates will be asked during the recruitment process to declare whether they have ever been investigated for, or found to have engaged in, gender-based violence in previous employment and/or in legal proceedings and provide relevant information to assist in determining suitability. Gender-based violence means any form of physical or non-physical violence, harassment, abuse or threats based on gender, that results in, or is likely to result in harm, coercion, control, fear or deprivation liberty or autonomy.

Not all heroes wear capes — but plenty of them use toilet paper

Want to challenge yourself at a purpose-led scale-up AND make a difference in the world? Come join the revolootion! We’re a leading eco-friendly household essentials business, and we’re a bit different: we donate 50% of our profits to help everyone gain access to clean water and a toilet. As of June 2024, we’ve contributed over $18 million AUD to this mission.

Basically, we’re really good at making it fun to do good. We operate scaling businesses in the UK, US, Canada and Australia, supported by an amazing team of 250+ people globally. Over the next 5-10 years, we’re aiming to increase our annual donation tenfold. That’s where you come in.

A bit about the role


As our Director of Pricing & Revenue Management, you are the master architect of our global value exchange. You’ll turn complex data into clear pricing and promotional strategy that ensures our rolls keep rolling into homes everywhere while hitting our ambitious commercial goals.

Operating as a leader within our Commercial Strategy and Insight team, you’ll be a powerhouse of data and insight-led thinking. You won't just be crunching numbers; you’ll be solving complex problems, developing clear narratives and driving strategic action across regional and global teams to ensure our growth is both rapid and sustainable.

If you worked here this past month, here are some things you might have been involved in:

Leading Price Transformations: Analysing market dynamics, scenario-modelling, recommending and then executing omni-channel pricing change, managing resources and collaborating cross-functionally to ensure seamless delivery. 
Shaping Long-term Strategy: Developing long-term pricing principles to feed into the 5 year strategy.
Promotional Analysis: Conducting a deep-dive on our last 6 promotions, delivering clear insights on what worked and what didn’t work, and translating findings into concrete recommendations and guardrails for future campaigns.
Testing & Learning: Ideating new ways to expand our promotional programme, and launching a promotional test-and-learn plan to validate hypotheses and find new ways to hit our commercial goals.
Retail Expansion: Partnering with regional retail teams and peer Commercial Strategy directors to prepare SKU and range proposals for new retailer entrances, building detailed cost and scenario models, finding the sweet spot for the shopper, the retailer and Who Gives A Crap, and translating that analysis into a clear range recommendation for the Executive team.

Let’s talk about you

You're a Commercial Visionary
You have a talent for building thoughtful models and enjoy exploring the "why" behind the data. But you don't stop there — you have a knack for translating that analysis into simple, persuasive narratives, and commercial strategies that stick. You're comfortable challenging the status quo and navigating difficult conversations with radical candor when the data suggests a more effective path forward.

You’re a Technical Powerhouse
Whether analysing past performance or modelling future scenarios, you thrive in the detail and have high-level proficiency with business analytics tools. You’re an expert in financial modelling, encompassing incrementality, ROI, manufacturing and logistics costs, price elasticity, unit economics and volumetrics. You can juggle multiple high-stakes projects across regions without losing sight of the long-term strategic vision.

You’re a collaborative problem solver
You value building strong connections across the business, acting as a trusted advisor and a supportive sounding board for leaders to help them navigate complex questions.

You have a wide lens
You understand the broad picture of an omni-channel, multi-market organisation and can optimise pricing and price ladders across different channels, in accordance with the channel roles, so that they make sense for consumers whilst hitting commercial goals.  

You give a crap

Do you want your work to make the world a better place? Do you want to work with other people who feel the same way? What a coincidence — us too!

Why should you work with us?

First off, as a certified B Corp™, we work to make a difference every day while maintaining the highest standards of social and environmental impact. With our most recent score of 125.5, we’re in very good company with other exceptional B Corps around the world.

We also believe that helping to make the world a better place should be rewarded accordingly. That’s why we offer:

Competitive, market-informed salaries.
Meaningful healthcare support for our global team.
Generous paid leave and tailored learning and development opportunities.
Free toilet paper (yes, you read that right).

Our team is really proud (95%) to work for Who Gives A Crap, and we couldn’t be more proud of that.

Have you made it this far?

If you’re still reading, we think there’s a strong chance you might be our kind of person.

Here’s the thing, though — research suggests that 60% of women and underrepresented folks might have already talked themselves out of applying. Even if you don’t check every box above, we want to encourage you to introduce yourself. We believe a diversity of perspectives makes a team stronger — and the stronger our team, the closer we are to delivering toilets and clean water for all.

Has significant experience in business analysis within complex projects
